cases.title: Herron v. Central Indiana Presbytery
precedent_refs: Case 2015-04: Thompson v. S. FL., M44GA
cases.synopsis: Complaint by TE Daniel Herron and others against Central Indiana Presbytery arising from a BCO 31-2 investigation and Presbytery's actions on May 14, 2021, including finding a strong presumption of guilt, suspending Herron from office, and releasing a public statement. The SJC denied complaints on investigative procedures (A), the suspension under BCO 31-10 (B), and the public statement (D), but sustained the complaint (C) that restricting Herron from Presbytery minutes and called meetings was a refusal of reasonable indulgence; the SJC also instructed Presbytery to proceed to trial and suspended consideration of related pending complaints.

cases.title: Eagle v. Savannah River Presbytery
cases.synopsis: Complaint by Eric Eagle and others against Savannah River Presbytery, found administratively out of order by the SJC Officers because the complaint was not timely filed within thirty days of the later of the SJC's notification (October 21, 2021) or Presbytery's ruling (October 20, 2020), as required by BCO 43-3. The case was dismissed on a unanimous 19-0-0 vote.
